mnemonics: ROI ABCs: Always Be Calculating (Return on Investment, Analysis, Benefits, Costs)
keyPoints: A business case justifies a project by analyzing its costs, benefits, risks, and alternatives.
Its primary purpose is to secure funding and resources by demonstrating the project's value.
A strong business case aligns the project with strategic organizational goals.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on technical benefits while ignoring financial or strategic business value.
Underestimating costs or overestimating benefits, leading to an unrealistic ROI calculation.
Treating the business case as a one-time document instead of a living artifact that should be reviewed throughout the project lifecycle.
